opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was just sitting on the couch and my heart suddenly took off at a million miles an hour, my chest felt tight, and I almost passed out before it stopped just as suddenly.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A sudden, terrifying episode of a massively racing heart with extreme dizziness that started at rest, lasted about 15 minutes, and then stopped abruptly on its own earlier today.

symptom_narrative.onset: completely suddenly, while sitting still on the couch
symptom_narrative.duration: about 15 minutes, then stopped abruptly - 'like a switch flipped'
symptom_narrative.character: a massively fast, regular racing/pounding in the chest
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: terrifying - felt like it was racing a million miles an hour, with a tight chest and nearly passing out
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: sudden onset at rest and sudden offset; this has happened three times this year, each episode stopping on its own
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing that I did - it stopped on its own, just like the previous times
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: nothing obvious brings it on; it just starts out of the blue
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: felt very lightheaded and nearly passed out (had to lie flat on the floor), and a mild chest tightness during it; feels completely back to normal now but very anxious about it happening again; denies actually fainting and denies severe crushing chest pain
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: three sudden racing-heart episodes this year, each self-terminating; otherwise very healthy with good exercise tolerance
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: fully recovered and independent now, but highly anxious and fearful of a recurrence, especially living alone

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; occasional alcohol; moderate caffeine intake and sometimes uses energy drinks/pre-workout supplements; the unpredictability of these episodes is badly affecting quality of life

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: frightened by the unpredictable, terrifying racing episodes and by being alone when they happen

communication_style.typical_message_style: anxious but coherent, describes the sudden onset and offset clearly

objective_data_offered.value: resting pulse now about 72 bpm and feels regular (self-checked at the wrist)
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-counted resting pulse now that the episode has resolved; she could not reliably count the rate during the episode, only that it felt extremely fast and regular
